framework.name: OWASP Top 10
framework.description: The OWASP Top 10:2025 list of the most critical web application security risks.

controls.title: Broken Access Control
controls.description: Authorization failures allow users to access functions or data beyond their intended permissions.
controls.guidance: Enforce server-side authorization checks consistently and test for privilege bypass paths.

controls.title: Security Misconfiguration
controls.description: Insecure defaults, excessive exposure, and weak operational settings leave applications easier to compromise.
controls.guidance: Harden every deployment tier and continuously verify configuration drift.

controls.title: Software Supply Chain Failures
controls.description: Weaknesses in dependencies, build pipelines, or component provenance can compromise delivered software.
controls.guidance: Track components, secure build pipelines, and verify the integrity of dependencies and releases.

controls.title: Cryptographic Failures
controls.description: Sensitive data is exposed when cryptography is missing, weak, misused, or poorly implemented.
controls.guidance: Protect sensitive data with modern algorithms, strong key management, and correct transport settings.

controls.title: Injection
controls.description: Untrusted input reaches interpreters or unsafe execution contexts and changes application behavior.
controls.guidance: Use parameterization, strict validation, and output encoding around all interpreter boundaries.

controls.title: Insecure Design
controls.description: Architectural and workflow design flaws leave the application exposed even when code is implemented correctly.
controls.guidance: Apply threat modeling and abuse-case analysis early in design and change planning.

controls.title: Authentication Failures
controls.description: Weak authentication flows allow attackers to impersonate users, reuse credentials, or hijack sessions.
controls.guidance: Strengthen identity proofing, MFA, session handling, and login abuse protections.

controls.title: Software or Data Integrity Failures
controls.description: Applications trust code or data that can be altered without verification, enabling tampering and unsafe updates.
controls.guidance: Verify integrity for code, packages, updates, and serialized or externally supplied data.

controls.title: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
controls.description: Insufficient logging, monitoring, or alerting weakens detection, investigation, and response to attacks.
controls.guidance: Log high-value security events with context and send them to monitored alerting workflows.

controls.title: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
controls.description: Poor handling of errors, timeouts, resource exhaustion, and unusual edge cases can create exploitable behavior.
controls.guidance: Design failure paths explicitly so the application remains safe and observable under abnormal conditions.
